title: Mapping fracture length using downhole ground penetrating radar
abstract: Ground penetrating radar (GPR) measurements from a downhole well tool in a wellbore are obtained to identify length of fractures adjacent the wellbore. A ground penetrating radar transmitter of the downhole tool emits an electromagnetic pulse. The electromagnetic wave of the ground penetrating radar is diffracted on encountering an end or tip of a fracture, which acts as a secondary source. The diffracted signal is then collected by downhole receiver(s) of the downhole tool. Length of the fracture is determined based on the time of travel of the electromagnetic wave from its emission until its collection as a diffracted signal by the downhole receiver(s).
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of determining location of end portions of a fracture in a subsurface formation penetrated by a well, comprising the steps of: (a) emitting electromagnetic energy from a ground penetrating radar transmitter in the well at a depth of interest; (b) receiving ground penetrating radar electromagnetic energy with a radar receiver in the well at a distance from the ground penetrating radar transmitter; (c) sensing time of receipt of the ground penetrating radar electromagnetic energy by the radar receiver; (d) determining from the arrival time of the received electromagnetic energy the location of end portions of the fracture in the subsurface formation. 2. The method of Claim 1 wherein the step of receiving ground penetrating radar electromagnetic energy with a radar receiver comprises the steps of: (a) sensing arrival of a first break pulse indicating the spacing of the radar transmitter and radar receiver; and (b) sensing arrival of a second break pulse indicating presence of a fracture as a secondary electromagnetic energy source. 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein the step of determining the location of end portions of the fracture comprises the step of determining the location of end portions of the fracture from the arrival time of the second break pulse. 4. The method of Claim 1, wherein the ground penetrating radar transmitter and the radar receiver are mounted in a well tool. 5. The method of Claim 4, further including the step of lowering the well tool to the depth of interest for performing the steps of emitting electromagnetic energy and receiving ground penetrating radar electromagnetic energy. 6. The method of Claim 1 wherein the fracture in the formation is formed by fracturing the subsurface formation. 7. The method of Claim 1 wherein the well is formed by horizontal drilling of the subsurface formation.
